title: 10 best herbal capsules for cough

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with cough because they contain glycyrrhizin, a natural compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. When inhaled or taken orally, glycyrrhizin soothes the mucous membranes and reduces inflammation in the throat and lungs, providing relief from persistent dry coughs and sore throats. Additionally, licorice root has been shown to have expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear out mucus and phlegm, allowing for easier breathing and a more productive cough. As a result, herbal licorice capsules can offer effective natural relief from stubborn coughs.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes in the throat, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with coughing.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in herbal licorice capsules have been shown to possess expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier for the body to expel.

Triterpenes: Triterpenes, such as glycyrrhetic acid, have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help combat infections that may be contributing to coughing, reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy respiratory system.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid saponin helps with cough by reducing inflammation in the respiratory tract and suppressing the production of mucus, making it easier to breathe and relieving cough.
Liquiritin apioside: This isoprenoid glycoside has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which help to thin and clear out mucus, making it easier to cough up and relieving congestion.

Licoric acid: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help to calm the mucous membranes in the throat and reduce irritation, thereby relieving cough and sore throat.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, helping to relieve cough and congestion.
Leaves: The leaves of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used in cough capsules due to their ability to reduce inflammation and soothe the respiratory tract.
Flowers: The flowers of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used in cough capsules for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to combat respiratory infections and soothe a sore throat.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 pounds of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots from a trusted supplier or cultivate it in a controlled environment.
Grind the harvested roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or a high-speed grinder for 30 minutes.
Combine the powdered roots with a binding agent such as starch or cellulose in a 1:1 ratio by weight to facilitate encapsulation.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with a portion of the powder mixture using a powder scoop or a funnel for 30 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in a cool dry place away from direct sunlight for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme capsules helps with cough because of its natural antibacterial properties, which combat the underlying infection causing the cough. The capsules' bioavailability allows for efficient absorption into the body, where thyme's active compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, work to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated mucous membranes. As a result, herbal thyme capsules provide effective relief from persistent and recurring coughs, helping to restore respiratory health and promote overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Thymol: Thymol, a key compound found in thyme, has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that help to combat bacterial infections in the respiratory tract and reduce inflammation, which can alleviate cough symptoms.

Carvacrol: Carvacrol, another main component of thyme, possesses expectorant properties, meaning it helps to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier for the body to expel, which can provide relief from coughing.

Essential oils: The essential oils present in thyme capsules, such as borneol and geraniol, have anti-inflammatory and mucolytic properties that help to reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract and break down mucus, making it easier to clear out, thus reducing the frequency and severity of coughing.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Thymol has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce the severity and duration of cough by addressing the underlying bacterial or viral infections causing it.

Caryophyllene: Caryophyllene is a sesquiterpene with anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which helps to relieve cough by reducing inflammation in the respiratory tract and promoting the removal of mucus.

Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid is a phenolic compound with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which helps to reduce the severity of cough by protecting the respiratory tract from oxidative stress and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part, as they contain the highest concentration of thymol, a compound responsible for the herb's medicinal properties.
Flowers: Thymus vulgaris flowers are used for their antiseptic and expectorant properties, which help relieve cough and congestion.
Stems: Thymus vulgaris stems are used to make herbal teas and infusions, which are often consumed to soothe coughs and colds due to their antimicrobial properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25 grams of dried thymus vulgaris leaves from a trusted source during the flowering season.
Dry the harvested leaves at 40 degrees celsius for 2 hours to preserve their properties.
Use a coffee grinder to grind the dried leaves into a fine powder within 5 minutes.
Mix 250 milligrams of the powdered thymus vulgaris with 250 milligrams of a binder such as gelatin or cellulose.
Fill gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them within 30 minutes to preserve their potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint capsules helps with cough because of their natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe an irritated throat and calm a persistent cough. The menthol in peppermint oil acts as a natural decongestant, helping to break down mucus and reduce phlegm production, making it easier to expel. Additionally, peppermint's cooling effect helps to numb the throat, providing instant relief from discomfort and itchiness.
remedy_properties: Menthol content: Peppermint contains menthol, a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory agent that helps to ease coughing by thinning mucus and making it easier to expel.
Expectorant properties: The menthone in peppermint has expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear out excess mucus from the respiratory tract, reducing congestion and relieving coughs.
Antispasmodic effects: Peppermint's antispasmodic properties can help to relax the muscles in the throat and airways, reducing coughing spasms and providing relief from persistent coughs.
remedy_constituents: Menthone: Menthone is a monoterpenoid ketone that helps with cough by relieving respiratory tract congestion and reducing inflammation, making it easier to breathe and relieving cough symptoms.

Menthyl acetate: Menthyl acetate is an ester of menthol that helps with cough by acting as an expectorant, helping to loosen and remove mucus from the airways, and also by reducing inflammation and relieving respiratory tract spasms.

Menthol: Menthol is a monoterpenoid alcohol that helps with cough by acting as an expectorant and analgesic, helping to loosen and remove mucus from the airways, reducing inflammation and relieving respiratory tract spasms, and also by providing a cooling sensation that helps to numb the throat and relieve cough symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most widely used part due to their high concentration of menthol and menthone, which provide a cooling and decongestant effect.
Rhyzomes: They contain a high concentration of menthol and menthone, similar to the leaves, making them a popular choice for cough and cold remedies.
Stems: They are also used due to their high menthol content, which helps to relieve cough and congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of fresh mentha x piperita leaves when they are in full bloom and the weather is warm.
Dry the fresh leaves in a single layer at room temperature for 7-10 days to prevent mold.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 1 gram of the powder with 5 grams of gelatin and 10 ml of distilled water to create a uniform mixture.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250-500 mg of the mixture and seal them tightly using a gelatin capsule sealer.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Garlic capsules helps with cough because of its natural antibacterial, antiviral, and anti-inflammatory properties. The compounds present in garlic capsules, such as allicin and diallyl disulfide, help to break down mucus and reduce congestion in the chest and throat, making it easier to expel phlegm and relieve coughing fits. Additionally, garlic's natural antibacterial properties help to combat infection-causing bacteria, reducing the severity of a cough and promoting faster recovery.
remedy_properties: Allicin: Allicin is a potent antibacterial and antiviral compound found in garlic that helps to reduce the severity and duration of a cough by combating underlying infections.

Volatile oils: The volatile oils present in herbal garlic capsules, such as diallyl sulfide and diallyl disulfide, have natural expectorant properties that help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, providing relief from coughing.

S-allyl cysteine (SAC): SAC is a sulfur-containing compound found in garlic that has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract and alleviate symptoms of cough.
remedy_constituents: Allicin: Allicin is a sulfur-containing compound that has natural expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, relieving cough symptoms.
Quercetin: Quercetin is a flavonoid with anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the airways, thereby alleviating cough and congestion.

S-Allylcysteine: S-Allylcysteine is a sulfur-containing compound that has been shown to have mucolytic properties, helping to break down and clear mucus, making it easier to cough up and relieve respiratory congestion.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are used due to their high concentration of active compounds like allicin, which possesses expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Seeds: They are used as they contain oil rich in gamma-undecalactone, which has soothing effects on the respiratory system.
Roots: They are used due to their rich content of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help in reducing cough and congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of fresh allium sativum rhizomes when the tops begin to yellow and fall over.
Dry the harvested rhizomes in a well-ventilated area for 7-10 days at 35-40 degrees celsius.
Grind the dried rhizomes into a fine powder using a mill or grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix 250 milligrams of the powder with 250 milligrams of a natural binder and 50 milligrams of a filler.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them immediately to maintain potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsules helps with cough because they contain compounds that soothe and calm the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and irritation. The capsules' anti-inflammatory properties help to ease congestion and make it easier to expel mucus, providing quick relief from coughs. Additionally, the antioxidants present in the capsules work to strengthen the body's natural defenses against infections, helping to shorten the duration and severity of a cough.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Ginkgo biloba's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce swelling and irritation in the throat and lungs, relieving symptoms of a cough.
Expectorant: The terpenoids present in Ginkgo biloba may help loosen and expel mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough up and relieve congestion.
Antioxidant: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant properties may help protect the lungs from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate respiratory issues like coughs.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds help alleviate cough by reducing inflammation and fighting free radicals in the respiratory tract, thus soothing and calming the irritated tissues.
Bilobalide: This triterpene derivative has anti-inflammatory properties that help in reducing congestion and inflammation in the airways, making breathing easier and lessening cough severity.

Quercetin: A type of flavonoid, quercetin is an effective bronchodilator that helps relax and open up airways, thereby reducing cough frequency and intensity by improving airflow and reducing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe a cough.
Seeds: The seeds are used due to their ginkgolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the throat and alleviate cough symptoms.
Roots: The roots are used due to their rich content of ginkgoflavonoids, which can help reduce mucus production and alleviate cough symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of dried ginkgo biloba leaves from a reliable supplier or cultivate them in your garden.
Dry the harvested leaves in a low-temperature oven at 50c for 24 hours to preserve potency.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or a high-speed grinder.
Mix the powder with 500g of gelatin capsules and 100g of tapioca starch in a large bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the ginkgo biloba powder mixture using a capsule filler machine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion capsules helps with cough because they contain compounds that have natural expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear out mucus from the lungs. The plant's anti-inflammatory properties also reduce swelling in the airways, making it easier to breathe and alleviating congestion. Additionally, dandelion's antiviral and antibacterial properties help combat infections that can cause coughs, promoting a faster recovery and relief from persistent coughing fits.
remedy_properties: Laxative and anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion's ability to stimulate bowel movements and reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract helps to relieve congestion and soothe an irritated throat, making it easier to cough up phlegm.

Antiviral and antibacterial properties: Dandelion's antiviral and antibacterial compounds help to combat viral and bacterial infections that can cause coughs, reducing the severity and duration of symptoms.
Expectorant properties: Dandelion's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to expel and relieving congestion and coughing.
remedy_constituents: Inulin: Inulin is a prebiotic fiber that helps soothe and calm the respiratory tract, reducing cough symptoms.
Taraxasterol: Taraxasterol is a triterpene saponin that exhibits anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, helping to clear mucus and reduce coughing.
Taraxasterol acetate: Taraxasterol acetate is a triterpene saponin that has been shown to inhibit coughing by suppressing the release of histamine and other inflammatory mediators in the airways.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are used due to their high content of mucilages, which help to soothe and calm the respiratory system.
Flowers: Flowers are used for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce cough and congestion.
Roots: Roots are used due to their rich content of inulin, a prebiotic that can help to regulate digestion and reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh taraxacum officinale roots and dry them in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 2 hours.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 50g of the powdered taraxacum officinale roots with 10g of gelatin in a bowl.
Use a capsule machine to fill empty gelatin capsules with the taraxacum officinale-gelatin mixture for 30 minutes.
Store the herbal taraxacum officinale capsules in a cool dry place away from direct sunlight for 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel capsules helps with cough because they contain a unique combination of compounds that work together to soothe and calm the respiratory tract. The active ingredients in fennel, such as volatile oils and flavonoids, have natural anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and irritation in the throat and airways. As a result, fennel capsules can provide rapid relief from coughing fits by easing congestion and soothing the irritated mucous membranes, allowing for easier breathing and relaxation.
remedy_properties: Antitussive properties: Fennel's natural oils, such as anethole, have a soothing effect on the mucous membranes, providing quick relief from dry, irritating coughs.
Expectorant properties: Fennel's fiber and essential oils help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to expel phlegm and relieve congestion.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel's flavonoids and phenolic acids have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling in the throat and lungs, alleviating discomfort and pain associated with coughing.
remedy_constituents: Anethole: Anethole acts as an expectorant, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, thereby reducing coughing.
Trans-anethole: Trans-anethole has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract and alleviate cough symptoms.
Foeniculin: Foeniculin exhibits expectorant and antitussive (cough-suppressing) properties, making it effective in treating cough and other respiratory issues.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Used for their carminative and expectorant properties to help relieve coughs and congestion.
Leaves: Used for their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties to help soothe coughs and calm the respiratory system.
Roots: Used for their expectorant and antispasmodic properties to help relieve coughs and congestion, and to calm the respiratory system.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of dried foeniculum vulgare leaves and flowers from certified organic sources.
Grind the dried foeniculum vulgare into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder within 3 minutes.
Mix 1 part of the foeniculum vulgare powder with 2 parts of a binding agent like gelatin within 2 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the foeniculum vulgare mixture using a capsule filling machine within 5 minutes.
Store the herbal foeniculum vulgare capsules in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger capsules helps with cough because of their natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the throat and airways. The active compound gingerol in these capsules has been shown to thin mucus and loosen congestion, making it easier to expel. Additionally, ginger's natural antibacterial and antiviral properties can help combat underlying infections that may be contributing to the cough.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger contains anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which help reduce inflammation in the throat and lungs, alleviating cough symptoms.
Expectorant properties: Herbal ginger capsules contain expectorants like camphora, which help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, relieving congestion and cough.
Antioxidant and antimicrobial activity: Ginger's antioxidant and antimicrobial properties help combat oxidative stress and infections that can cause coughs, reducing their severity and duration.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These iridoid compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation in the throat and airways, thereby alleviating cough symptoms.
Shogaols: Similar to gingerols, shogaols also possess anti-inflammatory properties that aid in soothing the irritated throat and airways, making it easier to manage cough.

Curcuminoids: Curcuminoids, particularly curcumin, have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce the severity of cough by alleviating inflammation and oxidative stress in the respiratory system.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of gingerol, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and soothe coughs.
Roots: The roots of Zingiber officinale also contain gingerol and other active compounds that help to relieve cough and congestion.
Buds: Ginger buds, which are the young shoots of the rhizome, are sometimes used for their medicinal properties, including their ability to help alleviate coughs.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes for their medicinal properties.
Dry the harvested rhizomes in a warm oven at 50°c for 24 hours.
Grind the dried rhizomes into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 250 milligrams of the powder with 500 milligrams of gelatin in a bowl.
Fill empty capsules with the mixture and seal them immediately to preserve freshness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile capsules helps with cough because they contain apigenin, an antioxidant that soothes the throat and eases inflammation. When taken orally, chamomile's anti-inflammatory properties work to calm the irritated mucous membranes in the respiratory tract, reducing congestion and relieving a persistent cough. Additionally, chamomile's sedative effects can help promote relaxation, making it easier for the body to recover from an illness that causes coughing.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce inflammation in the throat and lungs, which can ease the discomfort and pain associated with a cough.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in chamomile capsules help to protect the respiratory tract from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic coughing.

Sedative and relaxing properties: Chamomile's sedative and relaxing effects can help to calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety, making it easier for the body to recover from a cough and promoting better sleep.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: An apigenin is a flavonoid that acts as an antispasmodic, which helps to relax the muscles in the airways and reduce coughing spasms.
Matricaria ester (Matricarin): This compound has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate cough symptoms.
α-Bisabolol: α-Bisabolol is a sesquiterpene that has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, helping to thin mucus and make it easier to expel, thus relieving cough symptoms.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help to relieve cough and calm the throat.
Leaves: They are used for their medicinal properties, which include reducing inflammation and congestion, making them suitable for cough relief.
Seeds: They are used due to their expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, relieving cough and congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 dried matricaria chamomilla flowers for a minimum of 100 capsules.
Crush the flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Sift the powder through a fine-mesh sieve to remove any lumps or large particles.
Fill a capsule machine with 200-250 milligrams of the sifted powder for each capsule.
Seal the capsules using a capsule machine and store them in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stevia capsules helps with cough because they contain natural compounds that soothe and calm the throat, reducing inflammation and congestion. The herbal extracts in these capsules have antimicrobial properties, which help to combat the underlying infection causing the cough. Additionally, stevia's natural sweetness can help to coat the throat, providing relief from irritation and discomfort. As a result, stevia capsules can be an effective way to alleviate cough symptoms and promote overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Stevia's antioxidant properties help to reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, which can contribute to the persistence and severity of coughs.
Anti-inflammatory Properties: Stevia's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as stevioside and rebaudioside A, help to reduce swelling and irritation in the airways, providing relief from coughing.
Expectorant Properties: Stevia's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and respiratory tract, making it easier to cough up phlegm and relieving congestion.
remedy_constituents: Rebaudioside A: This compound has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate cough symptoms.
Steviol Glycosides: These compounds have been found to have expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough up.

Catechins: These phenolic compounds have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the airways, leading to relief from cough symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Stevia leaves contain glycosides, particularly stevioside and rebaudioside, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe a sore throat and calm a cough.
Roots: Stevia roots are a rich source of antioxidants and have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, including coughs, due to their expectorant properties that help clear mucus.
(Optional) Leaves: However, some sources may recommend using the leaves again for their antiviral properties which might help in reducing the severity of a cough.
remedy_recipe: Harvest stevia rebaudiana leaves in the morning when they are fresh and at their peak potency.
Dry the stevia rebaudiana leaves in a single layer at room temperature for 7 to 10 days.
Grind the dried stevia rebaudiana leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 50 grams of stevia rebaudiana powder with 50 grams of microcrystalline cellulose in a bowl.
Fill 100 to 200 herbal stevia rebaudiana capsules using a capsule filling machine in 10 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Japanese honeysuckle capsules helps with cough because they contain a concentrated extract of Lonicera japonica, a plant native to East Asia. The capsule's active compounds, such as flavonoids and saponins, have been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to soothe respiratory issues like coughs, bronchitis, and asthma. By reducing inflammation and congestion in the throat and airways, Japanese honeysuckle capsules can help alleviate persistent coughing, making it an effective natural remedy for cough relief.
remedy_properties: Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Japanese Honeysuckle Capsules have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract and alleviate symptoms of cough.
Saponins: Saponins in the capsules have expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing the severity of coughing fits.
Essential Oils: The essential oils present in Japanese Honeysuckle Capsules have antibacterial properties, which help to combat underlying infections that may be causing or exacerbating coughs.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing cough and bronchitis symptoms.
Rhizomes: Containing essential oils with antitussive properties, rhizomes help soothe and calm the cough reflex.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lonicera japonica leaves and stems, washing them thoroughly with clean water.
Dry the plant material in a warm dark place for 3-4 days or by using a dehydrator at 40°c for 12 hours.
Grind 30g of dried lonicera japonica into fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 part powdered lonicera japonica with 2 parts rice flour, forming small pellets that can be filled into capsules.
Fill the prepared capsule shells with 250-500mg of the herbal mixture and seal them immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Selfheal capsules helps with cough because they contain the herb Prunella Vulgaris, which has been traditionally used to soothe and calm irritated respiratory tissues. The capsules work by reducing inflammation in the throat and chest, thereby relieving congestion and discomfort associated with a cough. Additionally, Selfheal's natural antibacterial properties help combat infection-causing pathogens that can exacerbate coughing. As a result, herbal selfheal capsules provide effective relief from persistent and irritating coughs, promoting a smoother breathing experience.
remedy_properties: Antiviral and antibacterial properties: Selfheal capsules contain compounds like flavonoids and terpenes that have been shown to inhibit the growth of viruses and bacteria, helping to reduce the severity of a cough caused by respiratory infections.
Expectorant properties: The mucilage content in selfheal capsules helps to thin mucus, making it easier to expel, providing relief from chest congestion and productive coughs.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Selfheal contains compounds like rosmarinic acid that have been shown to reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, which can help alleviate coughing caused by allergic reactions or irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots help to soothe and calm coughing fits by reducing inflammation.
Rhizomes: With their high concentration of glycosides, rhizomes provide anti-inflammatory properties that alleviate cough symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh prunella vulagris leaves and flowers in early summer morning for optimal potency retention.
Dry the plant material using a food dehydrator at 35°c for 48 hours to prevent spoilage and preserve bioactive compounds.
Grind the dried herb into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for uniform dosing.
Mix one part of the powdered prunella vulagris with two parts of gelatin capsules in a small bowl for easy filling.
Fill the capsules with the prepared herbal blend and seal immediately to maintain potency and freshness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum capsules helps with cough because they contain eucalyptus oil, a natural decongestant that effectively relieves chest congestion and reduces mucus production. The capsules also possess anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the throat and bronchial tubes, providing instant relief from coughing spasms. Additionally, the expectorant properties of Tasmanian blue gum help loosen and expel mucus, allowing for easier breathing and a reduction in cough frequency and severity.
remedy_properties: Eucalyptol: Eucalyptol has natural decongestant and expectorant properties, helping to relieve congestion and expel mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough out irritants and reducing coughing fits.
Cineole: Cineole has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which help to soothe and calm irritated airway tissues, reducing inflammation and spasms that can contribute to persistent coughing.

Piperitenone: Piperitenone has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to relieve the discomfort and pain associated with coughing, as well as reducing inflammation in the throat and respiratory tract.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenoids, that help to soothe and calm the respiratory tract.
Barks: Contains alkaloids like eucalyptol, which has natural decongestant and expectorant properties that relieve cough symptoms.
Rhyzomes: Rich in mucilages and saponins, which help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the throat, making it a soothing ingredient for cough relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried leaves of eucalyptus globulus in the morning when dew is still present.
Clean and dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator or air circulation method for 24 hours.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 1000mg of eucalyptus globulus powder with 500mg of gelatin and 250ml of distilled water to form a capsule filling mixture, stirring for 10 minutes.
Fill empty capsules with the prepared mixture and seal them immediately using a capsule filler machine or manual technique within 30 days of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder capsules helps with cough because they contain active ingredients that target the root causes of coughing. The antioxidants and flavonoids present in elderberry work to reduce inflammation and congestion in the throat, making it easier to breathe and swallow. Additionally, elder capsules can help to soothe an irritated throat, reducing discomfort and making it more comfortable to cough out mucus. By addressing these underlying issues, elder capsules provide effective relief from coughs caused by colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
remedy_properties: Antiviral compounds: Elderberry's antiviral compounds, such as anthocyanins and flavonoids, help to combat viral infections that cause coughs, reducing their severity and duration.
Mucolytic properties: The mucilage present in elderberry has been shown to break down mucus, making it easier to expel from the lungs and airways, thereby relieving congestion and coughing fits.
Anti-inflammatory effects: Elderberry's flavonoids have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, which can contribute to a dry or hacking cough.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in expectorant properties, helping to loosen and remove mucus from the respiratory tract.
Rhizomes: Contain saponins, which help to break down and eliminate excess mucus, providing relief from coughing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest sambucus nigra flowers and leaves of good quality and sufficient quantity for desired dosage.
Dry the harvested material using air circulation or low-temperature drying method for 1-2 weeks.
Grind dried sambucus nigra into a fine powder using mortar and pestle or food processor.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with precise amount of ground sambucus nigra according to desired dosage and seal.
Store the finished capsules in airtight containers at room temperature for up to 6 months from preparation date.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hyssop capsules helps with cough because they contain the essential oils of the hyssop plant, which have natural expectorant properties. When taken as a capsule, these oils work to loosen and clear out mucus from the lungs and airways, reducing the severity of a cough and making it easier to breathe. The antispasmodic properties of hyssop also help to soothe the respiratory tract, calming coughing fits and providing relief from discomfort. As a result, hyssop capsules can be a natural and effective way to manage symptoms of cough and promote overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Hyssop capsules help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to cough up and relieving congestion.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Hyssop capsules have anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce inflammation in the airways, throat, and lungs, which can help soothe a sore throat and ease a dry or hacking cough.

Antimicrobial properties: Hyssop capsules contain antimicrobial compounds that fight off bacteria, viruses, and other microorganisms that cause infections, helping to prevent complications like pneumonia or bronchitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Hyssop roots are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help reduce inflammation and soothe a persistent cough.
Seeds: Hyssop seeds contain expectorant properties that help loosen mucus and phlegm from the respiratory tract, relieving congestion and coughing fits.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hyssopus officinalis leaves and stems, drying them in a warm place for 24 hours or less.
Grind 20 grams of dried hyssopus officinalis into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered hyssopus officinalis with 500 milliliters of boiling water to create a decoction, allowing it to steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, discarding the solids and measuring the liquid.
Fill empty capsules with approximately 200-250 milligrams of the strained liquid hyssopus officinalis decoction and store them in an airtight container for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yanhusuo capsules helps with cough because they contain the extract of Corydalus yanhusuo, a traditional Chinese herb known for its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. When taken as directed, these capsules can help to relieve coughs caused by respiratory issues such as bronchitis, asthma, and chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD). By soothing irritated mucous membranes and reducing inflammation in the airways, Yanhusuo capsules can help to calm persistent coughing fits and provide relief from discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antitussive property: It helps to relieve coughing by reducing inflammation in the airways, allowing for easier breathing and less discomfort from coughing fits.
Expectorant property: It helps to thin and loosen mucus, making it easier to expel from the lungs and chest, which can help to alleviate congestion and coughing.
Anti-inflammatory property: It reduces inflammation in the airways, which can help to soothe the throat and reduce the discomfort associated with a cough.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in alkaloids and glycosides, making them effective in relieving coughs and reducing inflammation.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce irritation in the throat and chest, providing relief from coughing.
Barks: The barks contain compounds that help to expel mucus and phlegm from the respiratory tract, making it an effective treatment for chronic coughs.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of dried corydalis yanhusuo roots and clean them thoroughly to remove any impurities.
Grind 20 grams of the cleaned corydalis roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix the ground corydalis powder with 50 grams of honey in a bowl and blend well until a uniform mixture is formed.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250 milligrams of the corydalis-honey mixture each and seal tightly to ensure freshness.
Store the finished herbal corydalis yanhusuo capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months before use.

intro_desc: Herbal capsules for cough are natural supplements made from plant extracts that help alleviate cough symptoms by soothing the throat, reducing inflammation, and loosening mucus. The best herbal capsules for cough include Echinacea purpurea, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Eucalyptus globulus, Thymus vulgaris, Zingiber officinale, Mentha x piperita, Ginkgo biloba, Lavandula angustifolia, Ephedra sinica, and Sambucus nigra. If left untreated, cough can lead to sleep deprivation, fatigue, and decreased productivity, impacting daily life and overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to cough that are treated with herbal capsules are respiratory issues such as bronchitis, asthma, and sinusitis. Herbal supplements like eucalyptus, ginger, and licorice root help soothe inflamed airways and reduce congestion. Additionally, herbal capsules may also be used to treat allergies, colds, and flu by boosting the immune system and reducing inflammation.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with cough is a blend of Echinacea, Ginger, and Thyme. Echinacea enhances the immune system, while Ginger soothes the throat and reduces inflammation. Thyme, with its antimicrobial properties, helps combat infections. This trio works synergistically to alleviate cough symptoms, reduce congestion, and promote overall respiratory health. By incorporating these capsules into your daily routine, you can effectively manage coughs and support your body's natural healing processes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea capsules helps with cough because they contain bioactive compounds that stimulate the immune system to fight off infections. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties in Echinacea purpurea help to soothe and calm the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and congestion. By boosting the body's natural defenses, Echinacea purpurea capsules can help to alleviate cough symptoms, shortening the duration and severity of a cold or flu. This natural remedy can provide relief and support the body's ability to recover from illness.
remedy_constituents: Ishwareones: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate cough symptoms by reducing inflammation in the respiratory tract.
Cichoric acid: This phenolic acid exhibits antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help combat infections and reduce inflammation in the throat, thereby alleviating cough.
Alkylamides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and immune-modulating effects that help boost the body's immune system, reducing the severity of cough and other cold symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in compounds that are believed to have anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids and alkylamides, which are thought to have anti-inflammatory and cough-suppressing effects.
Leaves: Include compounds like caffeic acid and chicoric acid, which may contribute to the plant's potential cough-relieving properties.
remedy_recipe: Dry the echinacea purpurea flowers by spreading them thinly on paper towels in a warm dry place for 2 weeks.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder at low speed for 10 minutes.
Mix the powder with a binding agent such as gelatin at a ratio of 1:10 powder to binder.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture using a small spoon or capsule filler.
Store the echinacea purpurea capsules in a cool dark place for up to 1 year.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra capsules helps with cough because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties and its ability to soothe the respiratory tract. The licorice root, the main ingredient in the capsules, contains compounds that reduce inflammation and irritation in the throat and lungs, making it an effective remedy for coughs caused by colds, flu, and allergies. Additionally, Glycyrrhiza glabra has mucilaginous properties that help to calm and protect the mucous membranes, further relieving cough symptoms and promoting overall respiratory health.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: It helps with cough by reducing inflammation in the airways and modulating the body's response to irritants, thereby alleviating cough symptoms.
Licoricidin: It helps with cough by having anti-inflammatory and antitussive (cough-suppressing) properties, which help to soothe the throat and calm cough reflexes.
Licorinic acid: It helps with cough by exhibiting antitussive and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to reduce cough frequency and severity.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their high content of glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, helping to relieve cough and soothe the respiratory system.
Leaves: The leaves are used to create a less concentrated form of cough relief, as they contain smaller amounts of glycyrrhizin but still offer some anti-inflammatory and soothing effects.
Flowers: The flowers are used to create a more delicate and less potent form of cough relief, often used in combination with other herbs to create a soothing and calming effect.
remedy_recipe: Extract 100g of dried roots of glycyrrhiza glabra using 500ml of 70% ethanol for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a container and discard the solids after 30 minutes.
Evaporate the solvent using a rotary evaporator at 50°c for 1 hour to obtain a thick extract.
Mix 10g of the extract with 10g of starch and 1g of gelatin in a glass container after 10 minutes.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them immediately with a heat sealer.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus capsules helps with cough because they contain the essential oil eucalyptol, a natural expectorant that loosens and clears mucus from the lungs. The oil's decongestant properties relieve congestion and make it easier to cough up phlegm, providing quick relief from coughing fits. Additionally, eucalyptol's anti-inflammatory properties help soothe the throat and reduce irritation, allowing you to breathe more easily and recover from respiratory infections and colds. This natural remedy is a popular choice for soothing coughs.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): Acts as a natural expectorant to help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, relieving cough symptoms.
Catechins: Have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing coughing.
α-Pinene: Exhibits expectorant and bronchodilatory properties, helping to open up airways and promote the removal of mucus, thereby relieving cough and congestion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Eucalyptus leaves are widely used due to their high concentration of eucalyptol, a natural decongestant that helps relieve coughs and congestion.
Buds: Eucalyptus buds are often used in cough remedies because they contain a higher concentration of essential oils than leaves, making them more effective for relieving coughs.
Barks: Eucalyptus bark is commonly used in cough remedies due to its rich content of tannins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe sore throats and relieve coughs.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 eucalyptus globulus leaves from a healthy plant and air-dry them for 2-3 weeks.
Crush 10 grams of dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder within 5 minutes.
Mix 5 grams of the powder with 5 grams of gelatin in a small bowl within 2 minutes.
Fill 10-15 empty gelatin capsules with 0.25-0.5 grams of the herb mixture using a capsule filler within 10 minutes.
Label and store the capsules in a cool dry place for up to 6 months within 5 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris capsules helps with cough because they contain the essential oils of thyme, a natural expectorant that loosens and clears mucus from the lungs and airways. The antimicrobial properties of thyme also help to combat infection, reducing inflammation and congestion. By soothing the throat and lungs, thyme capsules can provide quick relief from coughing, making them a popular natural remedy for respiratory issues such as bronchitis, colds, and flu. This can lead to a faster recovery and improved overall health.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Acts as an expectorant, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough up.
Carvacrol: Exhibits antitussive properties by reducing the frequency and severity of coughs, while also providing anti-inflammatory relief to soothe irritated tissues.
Caryophyllene oxide: Demonstrates antitussive and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to calm the cough reflex and reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thymus vulgaris leaves are primarily used to make capsules for cough due to their high concentration of thymol, a natural expectorant and decongestant.
Flowers: Thymus vulgaris flowers are also used to make capsules for cough, as they contain thymol and other flavonoids that help soothe the respiratory tract.

Stems: Thymus vulgaris stems are used to make capsules for cough, as they contain compounds like carvacrol, which have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that aid in relieving cough symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried thymus vulgaris leaves from a clean and well-ventilated area at room temperature.
Dry the harvested leaves in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 2 hours to remove moisture.
Grind 20g of dried thymus vulgaris leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix the powdered thymus vulgaris leaves with 10g of gelatin and 5g of glycerin in a bowl for 3 minutes.
Fill 100 empty capsules with the mixture and seal them immediately to preserve the herbal content.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale capsules helps with cough because they contain the active compound gingerol, which has natural anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. When taken orally, it can help to reduce inflammation in the throat, loosen and clear mucus from the airways, and alleviate cough symptoms. The warming and soothing effects of Zingiber officinale capsules can also help to calm a sore throat and provide relief from persistent coughing. This makes them a popular natural remedy for coughs caused by colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds help reduce inflammation and relieve cough by blocking the production of prostaglandins, which contribute to coughing.
Shogaols: Shogaols have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties that help thin and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough up and relieving congestion.
Zingerone: Zingerone has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe the throat and reduce the severity of cough by reducing inflammation and protecting against oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Zingiber officinale contain similar active compounds as rhizomes, which help in reducing inflammation and relieving cough symptoms.
Buds: The buds of the plant contain higher concentrations of essential oils, including zingerone, which has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help in relieving cough and sore throat.
remedy_recipe: Dry 500g of fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes at 40°c for 24 hours to remove moisture content.
Grind the dried rhizomes into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix 100g of the ground powder with 200g of excipient material such as starch or cellulose in a bowl.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with 250mg of the powder mixture using a powder filler machine for 10 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita capsules helps with cough because they contain the active compound menthol, which acts as an expectorant to loosen and clear mucus from the airways. The capsules also have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the throat and reduce inflammation, making it easier to cough up phlegm and mucus. Additionally, menthol's decongestant properties help to ease congestion and relieve cough symptoms, providing quick relief from coughing and congestion. This natural remedy offers a gentle and effective solution for cough relief.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Helps to thin mucus and provide temporary pain relief, making it easier to cough up.
Caryophyllene: Acts as an anti-inflammatory agent to reduce inflammation in the throat, which can cause coughing.
Eugenol: Exhibits expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough up.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part of Mentha x piperita for making cough capsules due to their high concentration of essential oils and menthol content.
Stems: Stems are a secondary choice for cough capsules, as they also contain essential oils that help relieve cough and congestion.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the third most used part, as they have a higher concentration of menthol than leaves and stems, making them effective in cough relief.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried mentha x piperita leaves in sufficient quantity for capsule formulation approximately 1 ounce.
Crush dried mentha x piperita leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix crushed mentha x piperita powder with a binding agent like gelatin or starch in a 1:1 ratio.
Fill empty capsule shells with the mentha x piperita powder mixture using a small spoon or funnel.
Store the filled mentha x piperita capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba capsules helps with cough because they contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm the airways. The flavonoids and terpenoids present in Ginkgo biloba work together to reduce inflammation and congestion, making it easier to breathe and recover from coughs. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant properties help protect the lungs from oxidative stress, promoting overall respiratory health and reducing the severity of coughing. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to alleviate respiratory issues.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help reduce inflammation and irritation in the airways, which can alleviate cough symptoms.
Terpenoids (specifically Bilobalide and Ginkgolides): They have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help soothe and protect the respiratory tract, reducing cough frequency.
Terpenoids (specifically Bilobalide): Bilobalide has a protective effect on the cilia in the respiratory tract, which can help clear mucus and reduce coughing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to soothe and calm the respiratory system.
Seeds: They are rich in bilobalide, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate cough symptoms by acting as an expectorant.
Roots: They contain ginkgolid and bilobalide, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce inflammation and alleviate cough symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Grind 500g of dried ginkgo biloba leaves in a coffee grinder to a fine powder for 5 minutes.
Mix 250g of the powdered ginkgo biloba with 250g of gelatin in a bowl for 3 minutes.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler over low heat for 10 minutes to dissolve the gelatin.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 0.5g of the ginkgo biloba-gelatin mixture using a capsule filler for 10 minutes.
Store the herbal ginkgo biloba capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia capsules helps with cough because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which soothe and calm the throat and respiratory tract. The active compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have been shown to reduce inflammation and ease congestion, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, Lavandula angustifolia has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate coughing fits and promote a restful night's sleep, ultimately helping to alleviate cough symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene helps by reducing inflammation and congestion in the airways, thereby easing cough symptoms.
Linalyl acetate: This terpene has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help relax the muscles in the airways and reduce coughing.
Caryophyllene: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help reduce inflammation and spasms in the airways, thereby alleviating cough symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their antispasmodic properties that help to soothe and calm coughs.
Flowers: Utilized for their calming effects and ability to reduce coughing due to their antispasmodic and expectorant properties.
Stems: Employed for their astringent and antispasmodic properties, which can help to reduce coughing and soothe irritated tissues.
remedy_recipe: Dry 500g of lavandula angustifolia flowers in a low-temperature oven at 40°c for 2 hours.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a high-speed blender for 5 minutes.
Mix 2g of the powder with 3g of vegetable cellulose in a small bowl.
Use a capsule machine to fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with the powder mixture.
Label and store the capsules in a cool dry place for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ephedra sinica capsules helps with cough because they contain compounds that have expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The ephedrine alkaloids present in the capsules help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, reducing congestion and coughing. Additionally, the herbs in the capsules have antimicrobial properties, which can help combat underlying infections that may be contributing to the cough. As a result, Ephedra sinica capsules can provide relief from persistent and stubborn coughs, promoting a smoother respiratory function.
remedy_constituents: Ephedrine: It helps with cough by acting as a decongestant and bronchodilator, reducing inflammation and congestion in the airways.
Sinapic acid: It helps with cough by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties, reducing the inflammation and irritation in the throat and airways.
Ferulic acid: It helps with cough by possessing expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, thereby reducing coughing.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are used due to their high ephedrine content, which helps to relieve cough and congestion.
Roots: They contain alkaloids that help to reduce inflammation and relieve cough symptoms.
Stems: They are used for their ephedrine content, which helps to open airways and relieve cough.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra capsules helps with cough because they contain high concentrations of bioactive compounds, particularly flavonoids and tannins, which have potent antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help to soothe and calm the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and congestion. The antiviral properties of Sambucus nigra also help to combat viral infections that can cause coughs, such as the common cold and flu. As a result, Sambucus nigra capsules can provide effective relief from coughs and promote a speedy recovery.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which help reduce inflammation in the throat and combat viral infections that cause cough.
Phenylethyl alcohol (PEA): This compound has a soothing and anti-inflammatory effect on the respiratory tract, which helps alleviate cough and provides relief from irritation.

Volatile oils (primarily geraniol, linalool, and borneol): These oils have expectorant and antimicrobial properties, which help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, reducing the severity of cough.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are used due to their high content of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties.
Leaves: The leaves are used for their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm the respiratory tract.
Fruits: The fruits, also known as elderberries, are used for their antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce the severity and duration of cough and cold symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of fresh sambucus nigra flowers or 250g of dried flowers from a trusted source.
Dry the sambucus nigra flowers in a single layer at 50°c for 2 hours using a dehydrator.
Grind the dried sambucus nigra flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder within 30 seconds.
Mix 5g of the sambucus nigra powder with 50g of gelatin and 50g of water at room temperature for 10 minutes.
Encapsulate the mixture using a capsule machine into 100 capsules with a 500mg capacity within 10 minutes.
